parameters-en.Time: Adjusts the length of time for the 6-tap delay sequence to repeat. When set to note values, Time follows the system tempo.
parameters-en.Feedback: Controls how many times the 6-tap delay sequence repeats. When set to 0%, the sequence is heard only once; at 100%, the sequence cycles forever.
parameters-en.Low Cut: Applies a low cut (or high pass) filter to the repeats, letting you remove the effected signal below a certain frequency.
parameters-en.High Cut: Applies a high cut (or low pass) filter to the repeats, letting you remove the effected signal above a certain frequency.
parameters-en.Mix: Controls the blend between the delay repeats and the dry signal. At 0%, no repeats are heard; at 100%, no dry signal is heard.
parameters-en.Level: Sets the overall level of the block.
parameters-en.T1 Scale: For multitap delays, each tap's time is some percentage of the master Time parameter. For example, if Time is set to 1.000 second, and T1 Scale is set to 10%, tap 1's delay is 100ms (or 10% of 1.000 second).
parameters-en.T1 Pan: Controls the panning of delay tap 1 between the left and right channels.
parameters-en.T1 Level: Controls the level of delay tap 1. To mute a tap, set its Level to 0.0.
parameters-en.T2 Scale: For multitap delays, each tap's time is some percentage of the master Time parameter. For example, if Time is set to 1.000 second, and T2 Scale is set to 30%, tap 2's delay is 300ms (or 30% of 1.000 second).
parameters-en.T2 Pan: Controls the panning of delay tap 2 between the left and right channels.
parameters-en.T2 Level: Controls the level of delay tap 2. To mute a tap, set its Level to 0.0.
parameters-en.T3 Scale: For multitap delays, each tap's time is some percentage of the master Time parameter. For example, if Time is set to 1.000 second, and T3 Scale is set to 40%, tap 3's delay is 400ms (or 40% of 1.000 second).
parameters-en.T3 Pan: Controls the panning of delay tap 3 between the left and right channels.
parameters-en.T3 Level: Controls the level of delay tap 3. To mute a tap, set its Level to 0.0.
parameters-en.T4 Scale: For multitap delays, each tap's time is some percentage of the master Time parameter. For example, if Time is set to 1.000 second, and T4 Scale is set to 60%, tap 4's delay is 600ms (or 60% of 1.000 second).
parameters-en.T4 Pan: Controls the panning of delay tap 4 between the left and right channels.
parameters-en.T4 Level: Controls the level of delay tap 4. To mute a tap, set its Level to 0.0.
parameters-en.T5 Scale: For multitap delays, each tap's time is some percentage of the master Time parameter. For example, if Time is set to 1.000 second, and T5 Scale is set to 82%, tap 5's delay is 820ms (or 82% of 1.000 second).
parameters-en.T5 Pan: Controls the panning of delay tap 5 between the left and right channels.
parameters-en.T5 Level: Controls the level of delay tap 5. To mute a tap, set its Level to 0.0.
parameters-en.T6 Scale: For multitap delays, each tap's time is some percentage of the master Time parameter. For example, if Time is set to 1.000 second, and T6 Scale is set to 100%, tap 6's delay is also 1.000 second (or 100% of 1.000 second).
parameters-en.T6 Pan: Controls the panning of delay tap 6 between the left and right channels.
parameters-en.T6 Level: Controls the level of delay tap 6. To mute a tap, set its Level to 0.0.
